questions.question: What is the truth value of the statement P OR Q if P is true and Q is false?
expectedPoints: The truth value of P OR Q is true if either P or Q is true.

questions.question: Explain the difference between propositional logic and first-order logic, and provide an example of how each can be used to represent a statement.
expectedPoints: Propositional logic is limited to representing statements that can be either true or false, while first-order logic can represent statements involving objects and their properties.
An example of a statement that can be represented using propositional logic is 'if it is raining, then the streets are wet'.
An example of a statement that can be represented using first-order logic is 'all humans are mortal'.
